title: The AI Guardian
subtitle: Final Boss · AI Foundations
description: You have studied the fundamentals. Now face the Guardian — an ancient intelligence that tests only those ready to advance. Prove your knowledge to unlock the next world.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Guardian.

challenges.question: What does LLM stand for?
options: Logical Language Machine
Large Language Model
Linear Learning Module
Low-Latency Model

challenges.question: An LLM generates responses by searching the internet in real time.
What is the primary thing an LLM learns during training?
options: How to memorize facts from Wikipedia
How to translate between languages
How to predict the next token in a sequence
How to execute code

challenges.question: AI hallucination means the model knowingly provides false information.
Which of these is a key element of a well-structured prompt?
options: Using as few words as possible
Providing a role, clear instructions, and the input
Avoiding any context to keep the model unbiased
Always asking the model to guess the output format

title: The Prompt Master
subtitle: Final Boss · Prompt Engineering
description: You have mastered the anatomy of prompts, learned to engineer context, and explored advanced techniques. The Prompt Master demands proof. Answer the challenges to earn passage to the next world.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Prompt Master.

challenges.question: What is the most important element to include when asking an LLM to produce JSON output?
options: A request to 'be accurate'
The exact schema with field names and instructions to return only JSON
A high temperature setting
A follow-up message asking for corrections

challenges.question: Few-shot prompting involves providing example input-output pairs to guide the model's output format.
Why does adding 'think step by step' to a prompt improve accuracy on hard problems?
options: It makes the model use more tokens
It externalizes the reasoning process, reducing logical errors
It activates the model's internet connection
It forces the model to use only verified facts

challenges.question: Chain prompting (using the output of one prompt as input to the next) is most useful for complex multi-stage tasks.
A prompt returns accurate output but in the wrong format every time. The best fix is to:
options: Use a different LLM
Ask the model to try harder
Add an explicit format instruction with an example of the desired output
Reduce the prompt length

title: The Automation Architect
subtitle: Final Boss · Automation Basics
description: Processes mapped. ROI calculated. Dependencies understood. The Architect tests whether your automation thinking is ready for the real world. Face five questions that separate conceptual knowledge from practical mastery.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Architect.

challenges.question: A task takes 5 minutes and runs 10 times per day. Building the automation takes 6 hours. How long until break-even?
options: After 2 days
After 12 days
After 6 weeks
After 1 day

challenges.question: Process mapping should be done BEFORE building automation, not after.
An automation calls an external API with no retry logic. The API goes down briefly. What happens?
options: The automation waits automatically until the API recovers
The automation fails silently with no alert unless error handling is added
The automation skips the API call and continues
The automation sends an automatic alert to the team

challenges.question: One-off tasks with high complexity are ideal candidates for automation.
What makes a process a poor candidate for full automation?
options: It runs frequently
It has many steps
It requires human judgment at key decision points
It involves data transformation

title: The Playwright Maestro
subtitle: Final Boss · Playwright Fundamentals
description: Locators mastered. Actions practiced. Assertions written. Debugging tools learned. The Maestro conducts the final test of your Playwright fundamentals. Five questions. No room for error.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Maestro.

challenges.question: Which locator is the most resilient to UI changes?
options: page.locator('.btn-submit')
page.locator('button:nth-child(2)')
page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' })
page.locator('#submit')

challenges.question: Playwright's actions (click, fill, etc.) automatically wait for the element to be ready before executing.
How do you assert that a page has navigated to '/dashboard'?
options: expect(window.location.href).toBe('/dashboard')
await expect(page).toHaveURL('/dashboard')
await page.assertURL('/dashboard')
assert(page.url() === '/dashboard')

challenges.question: The Playwright Trace Viewer can replay every action, screenshot, and network request from a test run.
What environment variable opens the Playwright Inspector when running tests?
options: DEBUG=1
PLAYWRIGHT_INSPECT=true
PWDEBUG=1
HEADLESS=false

title: The Code Constructor
subtitle: Final Boss · JavaScript Essentials
description: The Constructor builds and rebuilds programs from raw logic, and it won't let you pass without proving you can do the same. This battle blends everything from the world — variables, functions, conditionals, loops, arrays, and objects — into one final test.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Constructor.

challenges.question: Which keyword should you use to declare a variable that will never be reassigned?
options: var
let
const
fixed

challenges.question: A function only runs when it is called, not when it is defined.
What does `function double(n) { return n * 2; } double(6);` evaluate to?
options: undefined

challenges.question: How many times will `for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) { ... }` run its body?
options: Infinitely

challenges.question: `[1, 2, 3].filter(n => n > 5)` produces an empty array `[]`.

title: The Pipeline Overlord
subtitle: Final Boss · Advanced Playwright
description: The Overlord runs an endless automated pipeline, and only a tester who has mastered fixtures, page objects, data-driven design, parallel execution, and CI/CD can keep pace with it. This battle draws on everything from the world to test whether your suite is truly production-ready.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Overlord.

challenges.question: What problem do custom fixtures solve when several tests need the same setup, like a logged-in user?
options: They make the browser open faster
They let you define the setup once and reuse it across tests
They remove the need for assertions entirely
They automatically translate the UI

challenges.question: In the Page Object Model, tests should call descriptive methods like `loginPage.login(...)` instead of repeating raw selectors.
What does looping over an array of inputs with `forEach` and `test()` produce?
options: A single test that checks all inputs at once
One generated test per item in the array
A syntax error
A fixture

challenges.question: What is the safest way to avoid collisions when the same test runs in many parallel workers?
options: Disable parallel execution entirely
Use shared, fixed data for every run
Generate unique data per run, such as appending a timestamp
Run all workers against production

challenges.question: Playwright tests typically run headless in CI because CI servers don't have a graphical display.

title: The Gatekeeper API
subtitle: Final Boss · APIs
description: The Gatekeeper stands between you and a fully automated workflow, testing everything you've learned about endpoints, methods, status codes, request bodies, and authentication. Only a request crafted with real understanding will get past it.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Gatekeeper.

challenges.question: What does an API endpoint represent?
options: A random string of characters
A specific URL representing a resource or action
A type of error message
A programming language

challenges.question: A GET request is generally expected to retrieve data without changing anything on the server.
A request returns status code 201. What does that most likely mean?
options: The request failed validation
A new resource was successfully created
The server crashed
You need to log in again

challenges.question: What must you do to the body of a POST request before passing it to `fetch`?
options: Encrypt it
Convert it to a JSON string with JSON.stringify()
Convert it to an array
Nothing — objects can be sent directly

challenges.question: Including a valid Authorization header lets a server verify who is making a request before deciding whether to allow it.

title: The Architect Construct
subtitle: Final Boss · AI Agents
description: The Construct is built from everything an agent needs to function — a reasoning loop, tools, memory, plans, and workflows — and it challenges you to prove you could build one yourself, piece by piece, from the ground up.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Construct.

challenges.question: What capability sets an AI agent apart from a simple chatbot?
options: Using more advanced vocabulary
Being able to take actions toward a goal, not just respond conversationally
Running on more powerful hardware
Never making mistakes

challenges.question: Tools let an agent take real actions, like searching the web or sending a message, that a language model alone cannot do.
What is the purpose of an agent's short-term memory during a task?
options: To permanently store every fact it has ever learned
To track the goal, steps taken, and recent results within the current task
To replace the need for tools
To slow the agent down on purpose

challenges.question: If an agent's plan runs into an unexpected error, what's the best response?
options: Stop entirely and do nothing further
Recognize the issue and adapt — retry, change approach, or revise the plan
Repeat the exact same failing action indefinitely
Pretend the error did not happen

challenges.question: In a multi-agent workflow, the output of one agent often becomes the input to the next, so its clarity matters.

title: The Integration Construct
subtitle: Final Boss · MCP Mastery
description: The Construct links together every server, client, and capability you've encountered, daring you to connect an agent to the outside world the way a real integration engineer would — cleanly, safely, and with the whole protocol in mind.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Construct.

challenges.question: What is the main purpose of MCP?
options: To make AI models generate longer text
To provide a standard way for AI applications to connect to external tools and data sources
To replace human developers
To compress images for faster loading

challenges.question: In MCP, a host can run multiple clients, each managing a connection to a different server.
Which MCP capability is best suited to exposing live, readable data like a file's contents?
options: A tool
A resource
A prompt
A handshake

challenges.question: Why should an MCP server validate the arguments in a request before acting on them?
options: To slow down the response on purpose
To avoid carrying out incorrect or unsafe actions based on bad input
Because validation is required to start the handshake
Because the protocol forbids any checks

challenges.question: A resilient integration handles a failed connection gracefully — for example, by retrying or clearly explaining what went wrong — rather than crashing silently.

title: The System Overseer
subtitle: Final Boss · Software Architecture
description: The Overseer has designed and dismantled more systems than it can count, and it puts every challenger through the same test: monoliths, layers, patterns, scaling, and the judgment to design something that can actually grow.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Overseer.

challenges.question: What best describes a monolithic application?
options: A system split across many independent services
An application where all functionality is bundled into a single deployable unit
A type of database
A design pattern for testing

challenges.question: In a well-organized layered architecture, each layer typically only depends on the layer directly below it, keeping the dependency direction consistent.
What is a design pattern, in the sense used in software architecture?
options: A specific snippet of code copied exactly between projects
A general, reusable solution to a recurring design problem
A rule the compiler enforces
A type of user interface color scheme

challenges.question: Why is identifying the actual bottleneck important before trying to scale a system?
options: Because every part of a system limits it equally
Because improving a part that isn't the true limiting factor won't relieve the real pressure
Because bottlenecks never really exist
Because only the user interface can ever be a bottleneck

challenges.question: Good system design balances keeping things simple now with avoiding choices that would make reasonable future growth painfully difficult.

title: The Productivity Paragon
subtitle: Final Boss · AI Productivity
description: The Paragon has streamlined a thousand workflows and seen just as many fall apart from vague requests and misplaced trust. To pass, you'll need to show you can request well, verify wisely, and design a workflow that actually holds up.
objective: Answer at least 3 of 5 challenges correctly to defeat the Paragon.

challenges.question: What tends to make a request to an AI assistant more useful?
options: Leaving out as much detail as possible
Including a clear goal, audience, tone, and relevant constraints
Repeating the same vague request many times
Avoiding any mention of what you actually want

challenges.question: AI-generated drafts are generally a good starting point to review and refine, rather than a guaranteed final answer.
Why should you verify important factual claims an AI makes before relying on them?
options: Because AI tools are always completely wrong
Because language models can sound confident while stating things that aren't true
Because verification is illegal
Because facts never matter in research

challenges.question: What kind of work task is a strong candidate for AI assistance?
options: A high-stakes decision with serious consequences for people
A routine, low-stakes task that's easy to review afterward
A task involving information you shouldn't share with third parties
None — AI should never be used professionally

challenges.question: A good AI productivity workflow often starts small, with one recurring task, and grows as you learn what genuinely helps.
